---
title: "rotation_matrix_to_angles"
generated: true
description: "Recover Euler angles from a rotation matrix (2-D or 3-D)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.affine`](../nitrix-geometry-affine.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:rotation_matrix_to_angles -->
```python
rotation_matrix_to_angles(mat: Float[Array, '... d d'], *, deg: bool = True) -> Float[Array, '... a']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:rotation_matrix_to_angles -->
Recover Euler angles from a rotation matrix (2-D or 3-D).

Inverse of [`angles_to_rotation_matrix`](../nitrix-geometry-affine/angles_to_rotation_matrix.qmd).  In 2-D a `(..., 2, 2)`
rotation yields a single angle `(..., 1)` via `atan2`.  In 3-D a
`(..., 3, 3)` rotation (the upper-left block is used, so square affine
forms are also accepted) yields three angles `(..., 3)` for the
$R = X\,Y\,Z$ convention, with the gimbal-lock convention that
the first angle is set to zero when the middle angle has magnitude
$90^{\circ}$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:rotation_matrix_to_angles -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mat` | `Float` | `...` × `d` × `d` | — | Batched rotation matrices, `(..., 2, 2)` in 2-D or `(..., 3, 3)` (or larger, with the leading `3x3` block used) in 3-D. |
| `deg` | `bool` | — | `True` | If `True` (the default), the returned angles are in degrees; if `False`, in radians. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...` × `a`. Batched Euler angles, with a trailing axis of length 1 (2-D) or 3 (3-D).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:rotation_matrix_to_angles -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `a` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
